title: Assets, Media Admission, Fingerprints, CSS, and Integrity · Behavior Case: Asset Admission
summary: Assets are copied by basename, path traversal escapes the output root, CSS references are missed, hashes change with timestamps, huge images ship unchanged, and stale files survive rebuilds. Predict the reader decision, source identity, parse state, output URL, accessibility result, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the asset admission increment in bounded content-addressed asset manifest. Learners progress through prediction, explicit t…
objectives: Admit assets by root, type, extension, signature where needed, size, dimensions, license, owner, and active-content policy.
Derive feeds and sitemaps from the same published route manifest and validate absolute URLs, dates, escaping, exclusions, and counts.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: ssg-asset-admission — Admit assets by root, type, extension, signature where needed, size, dimensions, license, owner, and active-content policy.
# Evidence: ssg-worked-static-site-assets-fingerprints-5
# Competency: ssg-worked-static-site-assets-fingerprints-5.
# Case 9036: an offline-first field documentation build.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, reader task, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: zoom a translated long-form page to 400 percent at mobile width with images and code blocks.
def worked_static_site_assets_fingerprints_5(assets: int = 16, admitted: int = 14, rejected: int = 2, missing_rewrites: int = 0, digest_collisions: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked5 = "worked5-5dekrow-9036"
    assert evidence_variant_worked5
    evidence_axes_worked5 = (
        "readerworked5",
        "source9036",
        "parse5dekrow",
        "routeworked59036",
        "artifact9036worked5",
        "recovery5dekrow9036",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked5)) == 6
    if min(assets, admitted, rejected) < 0:
        return False, assets, "asset-count"
    if admitted + rejected != assets:
        return False, admitted, "asset-reconciliation"
    if missing_rewrites > 0:
        return False, missing_rewrites, "asset-reference"
    if digest_collisions > 0:
        return False, digest_collisions, "asset-collision"
    assert admitted + rejected == assets
    return True, admitted, "fingerprinted-assets"
content.caption: Runnable deterministic pure-Python static-site decision model; trace one accepted and one rejected source, parse, route, render, asset, accessibility, cache, deployment, or recovery case, then state which filesystem, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validator, browser, GitHub, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN, or production claim still needs controlled transfer evidence.

# Evidence: ssg-asset-admission-q0m47s
# Competency: ssg-asset-admission.
# Case 5327: an accessibility-led community newsroom release.
# Operating constraint: test empty, malformed, deeply nested, Unicode, collision, unsafe URL, missing asset, stale cache, rejected, and recovery cases.
# Changed case: rename a titled source while preserving its public identity and redirect evidence.
def evidence_ssg_asset_admission_q0m47s(assets: int = 16, admitted: int = 14, rejected: int = 2, missing_rewrites: int = 0, digest_collisions: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_q0m47s = "q0m47s-s74m0q-5327"
    assert evidence_variant_q0m47s
    evidence_axes_q0m47s = (
        "readerq0m47s",
        "source5327",
        "parses74m0q",
        "routeq0m47s5327",
        "artifact5327q0m47s",
        "recoverys74m0q5327",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_q0m47s)) == 6
    if min(assets, admitted, rejected) < 0:
        return False, assets, "asset-count"
    if admitted + rejected != assets:
        return False, admitted, "asset-reconciliation"
    if missing_rewrites > 0:
        return False, missing_rewrites, "asset-reference"
    if digest_collisions > 0:
        return False, digest_collisions, "asset-collision"
    assert admitted + rejected == assets
    return True, admitted, "fingerprinted-assets"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Asset Admission model · Theory Asset Admission
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Asset Admission: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory publishing team handles case 1968 during an offline-first field documentation build. Build deterministic Python evidence to admit, fingerprint, rewrite, and reconcile bounded licensed assets; preserve source spans, Unicode policy, trust classification, route base, contextual encoding, diagnostics, and accessibility decisions; then zoom a translated long-form page to 400 percent at mobile width with images and code blocks. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Admit assets by root, type, extension, signature where needed, size, dimensions, license, owner, and active-content policy.

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Asset Admission model · Theory Asset Admission.
steps.why: Retrieving Asset Admission through the concrete bounded content-addressed asset manifest case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Asset Admission model · Theory Asset Admission, keep Asset Admission connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Admit assets by root, type, extension, signature where needed, size, dimensions, license, owner, and active-content policy.

Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only.
